==English==

===Etymology===
{{rfe|en}}

===Adjective===
{{en-adj|-}}

# Transcending the distinction between {{l|en|subjective}} and {{l|en|objective}}, or referring to a property not of the subject or the environment but a relatedness co-created between them.
#* {{quote-book|en|title=Love and Compassion|page=73|author=Christie Walter Rinehart|year=2009|passage=The existential therapist must not only cultivate an understanding of transpersonal love; he must also develop a perspective that is neither subjective nor objective but, rather, one that is '''transjective'''.}}
#* {{quote-book|en|title=Limits of the Secular: Social Experience and Cultural Memory|page=80|author=Kaustuv Roy|year=2016|passage=This space, which is also process at the same time, is neither subjective nor objective, but could be called '''transjective'''; it simultaneously forms the basis of our orientation and action. }}
# {{lb|en|maths}} Corresponding to shifts of [[preprojective]] and [[preinjective]] modules.
#* {{quote-journal|en|year=2016|date=|author=Ibrahim Assem, Ralf Schiffler, Khrystyna Serhiyenko|title=Modules over cluster-tilted algebras that do not lie on local slices|journal=arXiv|url=http://arxiv.org/abs/1606.05161|doi=|volume=|issue=|pages=
|passage=We characterize the indecomposable '''transjective''' modules over an arbitrary cluster-tilted algebra that do not lie on a local slice, and we provide a sharp upper bound for the number of (isoclasses of) these modules.. }}
# Relating to [[transjection]]
[[Category:Transjective]]
[[sn:Transjective]][[Category:John Vervaeke]]
[[sn:Transjective]][[Category:Lifeism Dictionary]]
[[sn:Transjective]][[Category:Oldest pages before the refresh]]
[[Category:Pages]]
[[Category:Git synced pages]]
